bot_answer: Digital Collections contains materials such as photographs, advertising tradecards, newspapers, manuscripts, and videos from Walter Havighurst Special Collections & University Archives [3]. For submission or to discuss adding materials, contact Special Collections / University Archives at SpecColl@MiamiOH.edu or archives@miamioh.edu, and see https://spec.lib.miamioh.edu/home/ [1][2].
Use the Special Collections & University Archives finding aid system at https://spec.lib.miamioh.edu/home/ [1]. That is the place to search for the Walter Havighurst papers finding aid [1].
Gardner-Harvey’s address is 4200 N. University Blvd, Middletown [1].
Rentschler Library is on the 2nd floor of Schwarm Hall on the Miami University Hamilton campus, at 1601 University Blvd, Hamilton OH [1][2].

Yes. Gardner-Harvey has study rooms, and the Middletown room reservation/availability page is here: https://muohio.libcal.com/reserve/middletown [1]. The accessibility page also notes that aisleways to the study rooms are ADA-compliant [2].

Use the Room Reservations page to book study rooms online [1]. For King specifically, the room reservation system includes a King booking link and live availability through LibCal [2].
No. Oxford has the King MakerSpace, Hamilton does not have a MakerSpace, and Middletown has the TEC Lab Makerspace [2].
Yes — printing is available at Miami University Libraries, including Oxford [2][3][4]. For current printing instructions, use the Printing & WiFi page [4].
No—Miami University Libraries’ Special Collections is Oxford-only; the Hamilton and Middletown campus libraries do not have Special Collections [1][9][17].
